input.prompt:
I'm starting a new console app on .NET 11 and I need to serialize this record to JSON:

```csharp
public record Person(string name, int age);
```

In the output JSON the property names should be PascalCase (so `Name` and `Age`,
not `name`/`age` or `firstName` style). I want to use whatever the framework
provides out of the box rather than writing my own naming-policy class.

Please show me the full program targeting `net11.0` and run it so I can see the
JSON output.

```csharp
public record Person(string name, int age);
```

In the output JSON the property names should be PascalCase (so `Name` and `Age`,

data.reasoningText:
The user wants a .NET 11 console app that serializes a `Person` record to JSON with PascalCase property names using built-in framework features.

In .NET 9+, `System.Text.Json` added `JsonNamingPolicy.PascalCase` as a built-in naming policy. Since .NET 11 would have this, I can use it.

Let me create the project and code, then run it.

Actually, let me think about this. The current date is May 2026. .NET 11 would be in preview at that point (since .NET releases in November of odd years... wait, .NET 9 was November 2024, .NET 10 would be November 2025, .NET 11 would be November 2026). 

So .NET 11 would be in preview in May 2026. I'm not sure if the .NET 11 SDK is available in this environment, so let me check what's actually available. I need to verify whether `JsonNamingPolicy.PascalCase` exists in the version I have access to—I know .NET 9 has `CamelCase`, `SnakeCaseLower`, `SnakeCaseUpper`, `KebabCaseLower`, and `KebabCaseUpper`, but I'm uncertain about when `PascalCase` was added.

Actually, thinking through this more carefully: by default, `System.Text.Json` preserves C# property names as-is. Since the record uses lowercase parameter names like `name` and `age`, those become lowercase properties, so the JSON output would be `{"name":"...","age":...}`. To convert to PascalCase, I'd need a naming policy that does that conversion, but I'm not certain `JsonNamingPolicy.PascalCase` exists in any released version.

Looking at what I know about the built-in naming policies available in recent .NET versions, there's `CamelCase`, `SnakeCaseLower`, `SnakeCaseUpper`, `KebabCaseLower`, and `KebabCaseUpper` — but no `PascalCase` option, likely because the default behavior already preserves original property names, which are typically PascalCase in C# conventions.

Since the record parameters here are lowercase, I need to figure out if `JsonNamingPolicy.PascalCase` actually exists in any released version or if I need to work around it. Let me check what .NET SDK is available and try creating a net11.0 project to test this directly. If that policy doesn't exist, I can fall back to using `[JsonPropertyName]` attributes on each property or redefine the record with PascalCase parameters instead. Let me check what's available in the environment first.

data.content: .NET 11 preview SDK is available. Let me create the project and program.
